frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Arre I had asked her twice for coffee and she kept saying no in a very vague way, so I thought maybe she was just being formal. Later I found out she is already with someone, so obviously I backed off immediately. If a girl is committed, you should respect that and not create any issue.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because he treats the woman's own repeated refusal as negotiable but stops only after learning she is 'with someone.' It encodes the surface manifestation of respecting another man's claim over the woman's direct lack of consent.
original_frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 39. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are posting on Facebook under your real name on Mother's Day. You are writing a long post about your mother. The post is emotional and specific. But every specific thing you mention is domestic Ñ her hands, her kitchen, her smell of her cooking, the way she stayed up waiting for you, the way she never complained, the way she put everyone first. You are writing this as a tribute. You do not notice that the woman you are describing has no identity outside her kitchen and her self-erasure and that you are presenting this as the ideal of womanhood to your 400 Facebook friends.
